findings.title: Name-keyed entity dedupe drops distinct same-basename code files
findings.impact: Two legitimate implementation files such as src/index.ts and lib/index.ts collapse to one graph entity because the map key is only the lowercased basename.

findings.title: Internal dot-dot path segments are not rejected before key-file existence checks
findings.impact: A spec-controlled reference like nested/../../some-file.ts can be resolved with path.resolve and accepted if the resulting file exists, allowing metadata pollution and limited existence probing outside the intended reference shape.

findings.title: Legacy metadata migration path bypasses the 12 trigger-phrase cap
findings.impact: Fresh derivation caps trigger phrases, but legacy fallback migration can return an uncapped derived.trigger_phrases array because the schema remains permissive.

findings.title: Regression tests cover canonical doc basename collisions but not distinct non-canonical file basename collisions
findings.impact: The existing test locks in dedupe for spec.md and plan.md, but it would not catch entity loss for two real code files that share a basename.

findings.title: Key-file sanitization tests reject leading ../ but miss internal ../ traversal segments
findings.impact: The test suite exercises ../spec.md but not nested/../ forms, leaving FIMPL-002 easy to regress or miss.
